title: QoS-aware MAC-layer Resource Allocation for 5G/6G Networks
items: Designing flow-level scheduling algorithms for QoS provisioning in B5G/6G systems
Translating CN-RAN QoS semantics (5QI, PDB, PER, GFBR) into per-TTI resource allocation decisions
Applying both optimization-based formulations and reinforcement learning approaches

title: Distributed Resource Allocation in Wireless Networks
items: Addressing exposed and hidden terminal problems in dense wireless networks
Improving spatial reuse and overall performance by estimating states of neighboring devices
Studying Wi-Fi and cellular systems from a MAC-layer perspective

title: Cross-layer Resource Management and System-level Orchestration
items: Connecting lower-layer scheduling decisions with application- and service-level requirements
Coordinating transport-, PDCP/RLC-, and MAC-layer parameters for multi-slice allocation
Leveraging orchestration frameworks such as O-RAN and network slicing

title: Resilient Multi-RAT Connectivity for Edge and Mobility Services
items: Studying service- and power-aware fallback across 5G, Wi-Fi/local connectivity, NTN, and low-power wireless technologies
Measuring how heterogeneous monitoring and safety-control traffic behaves under staged power and connectivity conditions
Investigating RAT selection and coordination for maintaining service continuity during network and power disruptions

title: Decentralized and Resilient Network Infrastructure
items: Exploring how future communication systems can reduce dependency on centralized terrestrial infrastructure
Maintaining distributed service continuity when terrestrial connectivity, power supply, or central cloud access is disrupted
Connecting resilience, decentralization, and edge-supported service continuity as a long-term research direction
